Rematch é uma proposta extremamente refrescante dentro dos jogos de futebol, conseguindo se destacar ao misturar elementos arcade com uma jogabilidade focada em habilidade individual e trabalho em equipe. Ele não tenta ser apenas mais um simulador — ele cria algo novo.

Um dos maiores destaques é o controle individual do jogador em campo. Diferente de jogos tradicionais como FIFA, aqui você controla apenas um atleta, o que deixa cada decisão muito mais importante. Isso cria uma experiência intensa, onde posicionamento, timing e leitura de jogo fazem toda a diferença.

O jogo também brilha pelo seu gameplay rápido e responsivo. As partidas são dinâmicas, sem interrupções como faltas ou impedimentos, mantendo a ação constante do começo ao fim. Isso torna cada partida extremamente envolvente e cheia de momentos decisivos.

Outro ponto muito positivo é o alto nível de habilidade (skill ceiling). Rematch recompensa jogadores que se dedicam, oferecendo mecânicas profundas que permitem jogadas incríveis. Quando você começa a dominar o jogo, a sensação de evolução é muito satisfatória, com movimentos e jogadas cada vez mais refinados.

A experiência multiplayer em equipe é outro grande destaque. O jogo incentiva cooperação real entre jogadores, e quando o time funciona bem, a sensação é extremamente recompensadora. Jogadas coordenadas e gols construídos em conjunto criam momentos épicos dentro das partidas.

Visualmente e sonoramente, o jogo também impressiona. Ele entrega partidas intensas, com movimentação fluida e impacto físico que fazem cada disputa de bola parecer importante e realista, quase como um “highlight” constante de futebol.

Além disso, Rematch se diferencia por sua identidade única dentro do gênero esportivo. Ele se aproxima mais de jogos de ação do que de simuladores tradicionais, trazendo uma pegada que lembra títulos competitivos modernos, mas aplicada ao futebol.

Conclusão:
Rematch é um jogo ousado, divertido e extremamente viciante. Com gameplay rápido, foco em habilidade e forte trabalho em equipe, ele consegue reinventar a forma como jogos de futebol podem ser jogados. É uma excelente escolha tanto para quem ama futebol quanto para quem busca uma experiência multiplayer diferente e cheia de adrenalina.

En 2025, que un multijugador competitivo no tenga crossplay entre plataformas tan populares es una gran desilusión.

Voy a seguir disfrutándolo (incluso si eso significa tener que comprarlo otra vez en otra plataforma), pero me encantaría ver una solución para que todos podamos jugar juntos, sin importar dónde compramos el juego. Este tipo de decisiones terminan afectando lo más valioso de los juegos online: jugar con amigos.

Very fun skill based game with 0 stats or pay to win elements for gaining advantage against others.
All just about skills.
Game feels difficult at start but once you start picking up and master more different skills and team play, the game becomes extremely fun.
Best played with friends or at least using the in game voice chat with randoms.
Highly recommended for soccer fans and skill based game fans.

$30 and it still has a battle pass, with even basic hairstyles locked behind hours of grinding or more money.

The fun part? It’s soccer. The downside? Pretty much everything else they added (except for no out-of-bounds and the cage, which at least lets you bounce passes and shots off the walls).

The biggest issue? Zero ball physics when a player has possession. Your animations don’t matter, if the game doesn’t register your tackle input, you’ll whiff and take yourself out of the play, even when you clearly made the right move. Combine that with terrible servers and constant desync, and you’ve got a frustrating mess that sucks the fun out of the game.

And why on earth do we get a Ronaldinho × Balenciaga collab before basic crossplay?

Loving this game. It’s amazing how much soccer tactics and patterns of play translate over. Defenders get caught ball watching, press resistance is a real thing, and you can make a goal happen by dragging defenders with off the ball movement. 

There’s a few rough edges for sure. The game definitely needs dedicated competitive solo queuing for example, but the bones of this game are fantastic and most of my beef’s with it are things that should be straigh-forward to iron out. 

If this seems interesting to you, it’s for sure worth checking out, and if the above rough edges get to you, maybe wait a few months and come back. 

Also, I’m begging yall, learn how to pass.

reviews.review:
JOGO DELICIA!!!
Precisávamos disso!
PEAKMATCH

The good:
- The gameplay is actually really good and has an emphasis on an almost fighting game-like RPS when you have the ball. It is easy to pick up and hard to master. You soon realize that the best way to have possession of the ball is to NOT have possession of the ball. This means using short taps, rainbow flicks and passing to purposely "dispossess" yourself for brief moments in time, as you cannot be tackled if you do not have possession (your character will automatically avoid tackles if the ball is not glued to their feet).  You are not in possession if the little blue diamond appears on the ball when you are near it. Imagine you are just playing hot potato until a scoring opportunity arises.

Everything you do is done manually - no auto aim passes or shots - which makes every single goal more rewarding when you score them. There is counter play for almost every scenario as well which makes defending and attacking both feel good. Stamina management is done really well, as not managing it properly means you will lose out on crucial offensive and defensive plays. The lower your stamina bar is, the slower you run. 

There is also something called "Extra Effort" which is a small 2 second burst of top speed you can access at anytime regardless of how low your main stamina bar is. How you use extra effort can determine whether or not you a score a game winner or save the game.

The bad: 
- The netcode leaves much to be desired. The performance seems to be better now on launch than it was during the betas, but the same glaring issues still exist, including rubber-banding and no regs. It is very common for you to successfully perform a tackle and you get both haptic feedback in your controller and visual confirmation that the tackle was successful, only for your opponent to phase through you like nothing happened. This is even more frustrating when you are playing goalkeeper and a shot that you saved (with the same feedback) goes through you and straight into the goal instead.

- Slide tackles should be both more punishing on whiff and more rewarding on hit. There are plenty of times where an opponent will completely whiff a slide tackle who is running parallel with me and will still be able to catch-up as there is not enough recovery time for the defender. And it is just as common that a successful slide tackle won't down the attacker for long enough to actually be able to gain possession.

- I don't care for microtransactions as I am just here to play the game and I can do that, but the base price of the game is a bit steep if they're going down the route of selling cosmetics and battle passes. $20 or so or even going free-to-play would have probably given this game massive traction.

- 4v4 is the perfect game mode in my opinion as there is enough field coverage without feeling like there are too many bodies on-field (5v5 is just a dogpiling tackle fest usually, and 3v3 requires you to cover too much space with not enough people). I would like to see ranked game modes for formats other than 5v5.

- The worst part about this game is all the randoms think they're Bachira (they all have that same yellow haircut and are all equally dogwater). This person hogs the ball, doesn't know what passing is but knows how to rainbow flick (unsuccessfully but they keep trying) and so this is all they do the whole game. This person will never play goalkeeper and instead run out and expect someone else to cover, and the few times they do, they pretend they're Magikarp and use splash. Play with some friends so you don't have to deal with this type of person and you will win every game with communication and teamwork.

Overall 7/10 game but worth playing if you have a few friends and like skill-based gameplay.

Rematch is a breath of fresh air for the eSports scene. While I’m not usually a huge fan of PvP games, this one really surprised me. Every match feels unique, with gameplay that leans heavily on pure skill rather than luck or grind. It’s been genuinely fun to dive into.

That said, it’s disappointing to see micro transactions in the mix. They don’t ruin the experience, but they do put a slight damper on an otherwise stellar game.

I have been playing the game since you opened the game for a short BETA period. I used to be interested in football, but even though I am not interested in football now, I can play this game every day without breathing. The game is very good with the idea and the mechanics combined. Long gameplay doesn't get boring easily. The mechanics are not too difficult or challenging. With the right timing, you can do very good things. This is one of the features that makes me like the game. I feel like I'm in old productions like GGO Football, Victory Kick-off or Tsubasa. It adds a very fun feeling, but there are a few issues I want to criticise the game, I want to come to them.

  First of all, I would like to start with the positive things I see in the game. I'm very happy that you have kept a lot of fine and pleasant details such as the fluidity of the game, the ease of the mechanics, environmental factors, music, sound effects, holding your breath during the shot. It offers a real field experience. Being able to train in the lobby, ease of interface, ease of communication and goalkeeper player mechanics are really effective. It's very nice that you include a lot of such fine details and I hope you keep it up. You have also provided a nice flexibility in character customisation without being too exaggerated. I hope you will bring plenty of factual content. 

 I think some ball animations are still a bit troublesome. It's a new game and that's natural, but a more accessible bug report interface would be nice. Because I didn't see it, whether it exists or not.Another drawback is that some key combinations can be inconvenient or confusing. For example, it would be nice to have the flexibility to assign keys such as "G, Z, B..." instead of jumping forward with "CTRL + C". We need to be able to customise the settings on the keyboard a bit more.
I would also like to mention the passing mechanic, which is a mechanic that many players have problems with. Although it is a little easy mechanic with the controller, we suffer a lot when playing with the mouse. I think a slightly softer passing mechanics would be better. I also think ranked matches should be not only 5v5, but also 3v3 and 4v4.
It would be extra nice if we could enter the training ground, which we enter by pressing ESC while in the lobby, with our teammates in the ready group.

Of course, I also have content recommendations that can be added to the game. different ball colours and variants, card patterns related to real players, customisable titles or icons, entertainment modes (such as throw-ins, free-kicks, penalties and many more) and customisable fans/arenas. The current custom arenas are nice, but we can also customise extra content like fans, scoreboard, etc. separately.

I'm done for now. I hope I have given you some ideas. I hope it has been an article that will help you solve the problem.
Thank you very much for releasing this game and keeping the price affordable for the Turks. I am writing these texts after about 36-odd hours of fun. Good luck my friends.

I have to preface that I believe this game has a lot of potential. The core gameplay CAN be very, very fun.

With that being said, charging $30+ for this game, and then still having an in-game currency system for cosmetics, for the state that this game is in right now, is a joke.

The servers are not good. The game is still fairly unstable and crashes semi-often. There are no game modes. The ranked progression feels so underwhelming. The queue times are horrendous. The overall community and how people play the game (at this point) is awful; you'll experience good teamplay in about 10% of games. No crossplay functionality is terrible.

I cannot recommend this game RIGHT NOW. I wish game developers respected consumers more. This should be a lot cheaper than the current price for how little game there is here.

ЕСЛИ ТЫ ЕБАННЫЙ ШКОЛЬНИК ЭГОИСТ ИЗ БЛЮ ЛОКА, ИЛИ ДОЛБАЕБ У КОТОРОГО IQ ИВАНА ЗОЛО, ТО ПОЖАЛУЙСТА НЕ ПОКУПАЙТЕ ИГРУ, НЕ НАДО, ЭТО НЕ ДЛЯ ВАС, ИДИТЕ ПОКРУТИТЕ ФИНТИКИ НА ШКОЛЬНОМ ПОЛЕ РАЗЪЕБИТЕ СЕБЕ ЕБАЛЬНИК ОБ АСФАЛЬТ И ИДИТЕ ЛЕЧИТЬ БОШКУ. 

P.s. Если у тебя iq чуть больше 71, и ты помнишь свое имя настоящее, а не Исаги Еичи или Наги, то даю секрет из-за которого выигрывается любая игра [spoiler] Отдавайте пасы, чем чаще, тем больше голов будет [/spoiler]
Спасибо за внимание, надеюсь 1% игроков, которые остаются адекватными и в команде в этой игре останутся, остальным ебанатам соболезную и желаю вернуть игру, или не покупать вовсе

Не покупайте данную игру. Я купил игру за 20$ - не маленький ценник для подобного проекта. И за такой ценник вы получаете сессионку без системы репортов. Вы неизбежно столкнётесь с "тиммейтами", которые без причин начнёт специально бить в свои ворота. Вот вы заканчиваете игру в рейтинге за одну минуту со счётом 0:4 - все 4 гола вам забил свой же, а вы даже репорт не можете ему кинуть. Есть рассинхроны, разрабы игры, похоже, купили самые дешёвые Турецкие сервера. Контента в игре в виде шмотак - сейчас очень мало, магазин в целом пустой. В ЗБТ и то больше косметики было. Вообщем, повторюсь, не покупайте эту игру, если вы не хотите дать разрабам деньги за возможное будущее и у вас нет пати из 5 друзей. update: Игра стала хуже. Теперь запуская катку ты попадаешься с командой руинеров, вы проигрываете, ты запускаешь следующую и у тебя та же команда. Складывается впечатление, что в игре онлайн человек 100. Разрабом всё равно, они даже и не думают добавлять репорты или доджлисты. Замутили калаб с Пумой, будь добр купить эти шмотки за донатную валюту, не хватает же того, что ты саму игру за 20$ купил. Лучше Фифку купить за эти деньги. Анимации убогии, ты будешь постоянно задавать себе вопрос: "Как мяч прошёл сквозь меня?". Эта игра умрёт раньше, чем в ней закончится первый БП. Жаль, я не могу вернуть свои деньги.

UPD: Не покупайте игру, разрабам пох.. на неё, лишь бы вы донатили на шмотки.

So far this game is fantastic, especially for the price. I have a couple of issues but other than that the game has obviously been made with a lot of love and with football in mind.
It might sound strange but the game is basically just Rocket League but with people. The dribbling and movement on keyboard is a little bit robotic and takes a lot of getting used to, and I really wish they would allow you to remap controls as I'd much prefer to play this sort of a game with a pad. I believe that's coming in a future update though. Hopefully down the line they add more technicality to dribbling, more skills. A role queue if you're in a party as my group has a specific goalkeeper would also be nice!

My wishes:
Ranked 3v3
Some customisation options such as picking different outfits based on Away/Home
Nerf bankshots from over the top of the goal, I've seen some people say the game is built around this mechanic but I think more skill expression would be allowed through if they rewarded lobbed passes in the box.
The ball is kinda jittery on ping that isn't even remotely high, and there have been times that I've had the ball, clear with a shot and my character just spins around in a glitchy way and cannot move.

Overall it's a great game. Highly recommend

Okay, I wish there was more than just "Yes" or "No" for recommending.

The "Yes" is because this game is fun. In a similar way that Rocket League is. Which means it needs a large casual playerbase for me to continue to have fun.

The "No" is for the devs/whoever is running business side of things. The "vibe" is very heavily monetization. Of course I am not against them making their money, I paid $30 for the game. But majority of progression is behind a paywall after already paying just rubs me the wrong way. You really think it is better to ask me to open my wallet twice than to just make it $40 and give me everything? Nothing new was added from the beta tests beyond an opening cinematic and then locking some of the cosmetics behind paywalls/in-game currency. When will companies stop showing all their content in betas prior to release and add some excitement to release?! Along with announcing no crossplay (not even PC Gamepass to Steam) just a couple days prior to release feels somewhat deceptive to the preorder-ers, like me. I have also experienced weird network issues during the first five games I've played on release. I am least concerned about this because I genuinely think they will fix those issues.

Good game, bad business practices. Willing to change to "No" if the focus continues on bundled skins and less focus on gamemodes/content.

AND GIVE US CROSSPLAY!

The servers are extremely laggy, the actions sometimes give you a mini-TP forward, and the game crashes in the middle of matches every 2x3 or even disconnects you from matches without letting you back in.
I spent several hours in the beta hoping these issues would be fixed, but no, they didn't for a change.
You often have a super-long queue even though there are people waiting.
The colors of shoes, socks, gloves, and shin guards change to opposite colors when you enter a match.
The game isn't ready to be a "finished game," much less ready for sale. It's worth noting that the quality of the user interface is poor; you have items to buy in the customization interface, others in the store, and others in celebrations. Wouldn't it be more logical to put all the items in the store and allow you to filter by category?
If you read this, reconsider buying it.

Update: Things are getting worse in ranked matches. Crashes are more frequent, a lot of people are dropping from the server all at once, and there's a huge amount of latency variation. It's just frustrating and disgusting to play like this.

Update 2: It turns out that according to them, you have to delete the Runtime folder in local Appdata to fix some bugs. If they do that, they have to reconfigure everything in the game.

Update 3: After a patch, the game is now running worse. The servers lag even more, and every 2v3, your entire team is kicked out, leaving you in a 1v5 or a 1v1 with goalkeepers because no one else can join the matches since the rest of the players appear in the roster but not on the field.

ALSO, YOU CAN'T PLAY WITH ANYONE ON GAMEPASS/XBOX OR PLAYSTATION BECAUSE THERE'S NO CROSSPLAY.
With how bad the game is, they should reward those who pre-purchased it and trusted them.

Update 4: It's July 19th (1 month since the game's release) and they still have the worst netcode in history. Disgusting desyncs where you see yourself taking the ball, but out of nowhere it disappears and the opponent has it (and it's not just you who see it, your teammates see it pass by too). It's unacceptable that because other players are lagging, they run all over the place and take the ball away from you. The servers have a constant ping of 190, but for any other game, the connections are fine. It's unacceptable that the game crashes an entire team, you lose, and even though it's the game's fault, they penalize you. It also doesn't make any sense for someone to leave a ranked match and not get penalized; they just have to wait for the team they were on to finish their match.

Update 5: You can now bodyblock the goalkeeper so they can't move when they shoot at goal. The netcode is worse than before, because you take the ball away from someone and it starts spinning around their foot as if the ball had a magnet. It makes no sense that with each update, they worsen the gameplay experience and never fix the netcode. Ball priority is becoming less and less understood. Before, if you had the effort active, you had priority. Now, everyone just goes to the ball, regardless of whether they have the effort or even the advantageous position in front of the ball.

I insist that the game can't be that bad; it wasn't that bad in the beta, either.

Update 6: Honestly, this patch doesn't fix anything that really matters.
The changes are merely superficial tweaks: volleys remain the same, 50/50s still feel unfair, and the "Extra Effort" still defines plays too much. Defenders still lack real tools.
The weakest point is that the issues the community has been highlighting for a while remain the same. This patch feels more like a "last-minute" than a real solution. As long as the underlying mechanics aren't addressed, the meta will remain the same, unhealthy for the future of the game and frustrating.

Update 7, post-Patch 3, now making saves is even more complicated. The goalkeeper increasingly makes the animation of grabbing the ball, but cancels it when it's right next to him. If you try to react to a dive, that canceled animation leaves your character on its side, so it becomes super counterintuitive to dive and at least try to hit the ball to clear it. THIS WAS SUPPOSED TO BE FIXED WITH PATCH 3, but they didn't. It's no wonder how bad the game is. We still don't have a system to report players who lose on purpose, disconnect after the first goal, or just bother their teammates and do nothing. Let's be honest, submitting a Steam account for trolling is completely unworkable. They're not going to do anything, and the fact that you can leave the game and your only penalty is having to wait for the game to end is an insult to those who actually want to play.

Teammates spielen nie ab, leaven das Game, machen Eigentore, im Voicechat werden Beleidigungen gerufen oder es werden Störgeräusche verursacht. Reporten kann man ingame bisher nicht.

Das Spiel selbst hat auch Probleme. Wenn ein gegnerischer Spieler eine miese Verbindung hat lagged er über's Spielfeld. So kann man gar nicht mehr abschätzen wo er gerade ist, wann er schießt oder passt. 
Zudem sind die Animationen und die Steuerung sehr clunky - es sieht immer nach Gewusel und Chaos aus wenn alle Spieler zum Ball rennen.

Ich hatte mir viel mehr vom Spiel erhofft, aber für mich war's das erstmal. Vielleicht schau ich nochmal rein, wenn das Spiel ein paar Monate alt ist.

EXCELENTE JUEGO, cuesta acosntumbrarse y en saber como jugar pero muy buenos graficos y bastante llamativo, solo mejoraria el tema de los servidores al momento de cargar una partida, si no se completa en ambos equipos no se deberia jugar... pero 10/10

Sería genial que implementaran un sistema de reporte para AFKs y trolls. No tiene sentido que marque 4 goles, haga 5 atajadas, más de 20.000 puntos… y aun así pierda porque un compañero se va AFK o decide trolear.

Soy un jugador que disfruta los juegos competitivos en solitario, me gusta superarme y mejorar, pero es imposible subir de rango si no tienes un equipo completo. Además, no existe ningún tipo de protección de puntos para los jugadores que realmente se esfuerzan.

El resultado es un ciclo sin fin: ganas un partido, pierdes dos; ganas dos, pierdes tres… y terminas atrapado en los mismos rangos bajos con los mismos jugadores, sin progreso real.

Tal vez algunos digan que “el fútbol es así”, que hay jugadores que destacan en equipos que no dan la talla y aun así pierden. Pero esto no es un simulador de fútbol realista, es un videojuego competitivo, y debería ser justo y balanceado, sobre todo considerando su precio tan alto.

Un sistema que reconozca el rendimiento individual —por ejemplo, si un jugador mete 3 goles y realiza varias atajadas— podría al menos ajustar la pérdida de puntos de manera más justa.

En resumen: mal balance + AFKs + trolls = un juego competitivo que se muere solo.

Ojalá los desarrolladores consideren esto, porque el juego tiene un potencial enorme, pero el sistema actual castiga a quienes realmente quieren mejorar y competir limpiamente.

I spent 10 minutes in the menus trying to find the button for 'pass' because they decided to create their own jargon for simple actions everyone in every country in the world knows by another name. Glitches from the beta are still pervasive, so in its current state the game is just a buggy, awkward mess. If you are a rocket league, fifa, or any other game fan, DO NOT grab this game in its current state.

Legendary Football and Football Fusion with AAA studio funding.
Although the previously mentioned titles are Roblox American Football games, the concept of a team-based sports game with mechanics that offer high skill ceilings and require team coordination not just on a "Madden" level, but on a peer-to-peer level, is so inviting it's hard to use never being into "soccer" as an excuse not to try it.

Seriously though, this game is so much fun that I've started to consider calling the sport football. I'm so excited to see what inspiration this brings into sports games, but for now this game has my attention. Good job Sloclap.

Comecei a jogar meio na dúvida, achei que ia ser mais um jogo que toda a gente elogia mas depois é só hype. Mas afinal... fiquei preso. O mundo é gigante e dá mesmo vontade de explorar — não há setinhas nem tutoriais chatos, simplesmente vais andando e descobres coisas por ti. E quando descobres... é daquelas experiências que te faz parar e dizer “wtf, isto é mesmo fixe”.

O combate é lixado, mas justo. Morres? Foi porque fizeste porcaria. Quando começas a perceber os padrões e a dar a volta aos inimigos mais difíceis, a sensação é brutal. Não é daqueles jogos que te pega na mão — aqui, tu aprendes na prática, à força, mas vale a pena.

Visualmente é dos jogos mais bonitos e criativos que já joguei. Há zonas que parecem saídas de um pesadelo, outras que parecem pinturas. E a banda sonora? Dá mesmo aquele clima épico sem ser forçada.

Se há pontos negativos? Talvez a história — está lá, mas não é dada de bandeja. Tens de ir atrás, prestar atenção e montar o puzzle. E sim, às vezes o jogo podia explicar um bocadinho melhor as mecânicas.

No geral, surpreendeu-me. É desafiante, envolvente e tem uma alma diferente da maioria dos jogos que andam aí.

Nota final: 9.5/10
Não é para todos, mas se entrares no ritmo... vais perceber o porquê do hype.

Ursprünglich hat mich das Spiel gar nicht interessiert – ich habe sogar die Beta komplett ignoriert. Erst ein paar Tage vor Release habe ich mir den Trailer angeschaut, und obwohl ich keine großen Erwartungen hatte, habe ich es vorbestellt und konnte vorab loslegen.

Die erste Stunde war ehrlich gesagt frustrierend. Ich hatte keine Ahnung, wie man sich richtig bewegt oder den Ball kontrolliert. Allein einen Schuss abzuwehren war für mich schon ein Erfolg. Aber dann hat es Klick gemacht: Je mehr ich gespielt habe, desto besser wurde ich – und desto mehr Spaß hatte ich.

Mit Übung und etwas Geduld habe ich angefangen, das Spiel wirklich zu verstehen. Und seitdem es "klick" gemacht hat, bin ich richtig drin. Es ist erfrischend anders, ein Arcade-Fußballspiel mit viel Eigenständigkeit und echtem Potenzial.

Was ich besonders mag: Nach jeder Runde denke ich mir – "Wow, das hat richtig Bock gemacht, ich will gleich nochmal!" Genau dieses Gefühl schaffen nicht viele Spiele.

Fazit: Ein absolut empfehlenswertes Spiel, vor allem wenn man bereit ist, etwas Zeit zu investieren. Für mich eine echte Überraschung!

Fifa Volta meets Rocket League
This is the soccer game I've been waiting for.

I really want to like this game. 

But aside from the shooting mechanics and how to approach them, there is not enough depth to the game. 
We dont need yellow or red cards for tackling, but no free kick at all for wrongful tackle from behind? no Penalty kick at all? I understand this game is inspired by Rocket League and Fifa Street but it lacks the dribbling depth of both games and the depth of real football.
There is almost zero chance that a goalkeeper just looses the ball for a second chance goal kick. You either save as a keeper or you don´t 99% of the time. 
This might be simply not in the game because the game engine can not handle this amount of free form ball kicking. The fighting in Sifu also felt like on rails with how the inputs are managed. 

Why is there no auto lock on for the ball? so that we can track it better? (Goal keepers should not have this feature) you always have to look at the mini map or you either need to handle a quick 90 to 180 degree turn. A trade off could simply be that the kicks are less precise with auto lock on. 

Tackling feels not as smooth as it should be because of the desyncs. 

Character Customization is superficially androgynous. 
You do not have the ability to make a good looking female or male. You want a good looking female footballer like in the real world? no chance. 
You want to have several male footballer in various shapes and forms with different heights and more manly faces and haircuts? Not a chance. 

Instead of prosthetics and lipstick they should have added more haircuts, more faces, more ways to customize the male and female bodies. 

The game is fun but it will clearly lack steam for ongoing support from the casual playerbase after like 40-50 hours max. 

It is a shame. I will refund for now.

Had to return until MnK controls are fine-tuned. More passes go to where they want than where I want them to go. Passing feels like a lootbox and after the unteenth time of getting slammed by a granted because the pass went to the middle of nowhere, I realized from a MnK perspective at least, this game is just not ready. 

Great idea held back by extremely clunky controls and animations that make the characters feel like they’re on rails. The transition between angles feels abrupt and the implementation of their passing control is borderline unusable.

Will check back because it shows promise, but from my perspective after the very minimal changes through ~6 months of beta, it’s gonna be years before any major implementation’s will be made. Fingers crossed!

[h2]The Good[/h2]
The game is very fun. It fulfills the football fantasy, empowering the fun things about football (passing, vision, playmaking, goals), whilst reducing or removing the less fun parts (out of bounds, throw ins, cards/officiating). The lack of off-sides is a neutral thing to me, it allows cherry picking, but reduces the barrier to entry. The game is easy to read and understand, and you don't have to know football to pick up the game and play. That being said, if you do know football fundamentals, you can apply them to the game. Positioning, controlling space, cutting off passing lanes, when to commit to a tackle or hold off, all real knowledge that can be applied.

[h2]The Bad[/h2]
The game is missing some important social features and quality of life. Rematching (ironically), better quick communication options, better voice options (voice activity rather than open mic), the ability to mute specific players rather than your whole team, being able to mute mid-match. I haven't played ranked, but it seems bare-bones. No crossplay on release is a big negative mark, though it is confirmed to be in progress and top priority.

[h2]The Ugly[/h2]
Server stability, and desync. Sloclap have released patches since early release that have significantly improved server stability for me, fixed crashes I was getting, and got rid of the worst of the bugs. That being said, stability and desync are still problems. Not in every match and every instance, but often enough to be annoying.

[h2]Conclusion[/h2]
The core of the game is great. If the developers had taken an extra 6 months I think they could have released extremely strong. Instead they have a flawed gem that they will need to polish. The devs have released good updates since early release, are communicating well, and have their priorities straight. Despite being rough around the edges, this makes me hopeful for the games future. I love this game, and want it to succeed, which might make me more tolerant of the game's problems than others would be. If you want a polished experience, check back in 6 months. If you want to have some fun, and don't mind the occasional desync, consider buying.

Caveat: Wait for a week, maybe two. Game currently crashes during a goal replay *very* often and is suffering networking problems, to the point the game is borderline unplayable. Happened two matches in a row before I could finally play a game to completion. :/


There is also no cross-play between pc and consoles (with the exception of PCGamepass and Xbox Gamepass, which are on the same platform - but the PCGamepass players can't play with Steam players), which is strange given the game is meant to be played on controller and PC players don't really have a significant advantage here - then again it's probably for the best right now, given that it's hard to even get matchmade into a game because of networking problems ("Game is too popular" per the devs). 
Crossplay is a promised feature that will show up eventually, and knowing how quickly gamers shift from one game to the next may actually be a nice refresh of the game's community to a small extent - depending on when it arrives.

Be aware: The discord they have for public relations and tech support is middling in its usefulness. 

Otherwise: This game is a solid arcade-y soccer game that has potential for a significant skill ceiling that can make the game really exciting to both play and watch. 
At present it is marred by technical difficulties that are sometimes out of the devs control (popularity is crushing the service provider they're using, causing networking and matchmaking issues) and some within (game crashing during goal celebrations and replays). 
My excitement for the game is presently deflated due to the above, but I have hope the dev team will rebound quickly and get things sorted.

[strike]Queue times can take longer than 10 minutes.[/strike]

Desync and logic inconsistencies, detrimental to a game centered around timing, ball prompting, tackling, and control. 

Anything higher than 55ms ping and the ball will be teleporting occassionally.

There is no reconnect on crash feature. About half of the 5s ranked games are one man down or more.

The game crashes for many, randomly. 
Pricing: 30 dollars but plays like a 10 dollar game on top of a F2P microtransaction system. 

The microtransaction system is pretty lackluster.

Kits are client side, but opposing team gets generated a random kit loadout instead of wearing your opposite kit.

Stadiums for some reason are NOT client side, and one stadium (desert) will flashbang your eyes to hell and back.

Lack of communication options in the wheel

No option to report/individually mute.

Teammates can really make or break the feel of a match. Try your best not to play solo.

Honestly, needs an honor system that shadow bans players into queuing with each other. 

Lastly, it's an arena PVP game. Historically, these don't last very long, even the genuinely really fun ones. Needed more time in the oven I'm afraid, more players are gonna leave sooner than later.

Great game to play with friends!

The game is fun. More fun with 3-5 friends as any online competitive game is better with coordination and team play, but solo queue can still be enjoyable if you focus on your own individual game play. It definitely gives you that "One. More. Game." feeling wanting for more, and time can pass pretty quickly while playing. 

There are netcode/latency issues as well as some game crashes that I am sure will be fleshed out within due time. If that's a deal breaker for you, just wait  a bit for some early hot fixes. Also, if you are controller deficient like myself (I have to use KBM), the passing is a bit awkward at times. They want you to use the mouse like a joystick to aim your passes yet that's not really intuitive at all. I rather just whip my mouse and aim where I need to aim. An example of an issue is if you're in the middle of rotating your camera and then hit the pass button, your pass will go in some random direction. I believe they are working on giving the player a few different options regarding how passing will work. It has improved some already though. 

Overall, it's worth the $30, but I am quite surprised they didn't go with a Free to play model with more emphasis on a cash shop and cosmetics. The stylized graphics can make for some fun outfits and appearances. Even with it's limited selection, I find myself changing my appearance quite often, and I am typically someone that has no care in the world for skins.

If you're on the fence, just grab it and give it 2 hours of gameplay to see if it's something for you.

Even at Master rank (second highest rank) ball-hogs are a serious issue. Had a guy in a 6 min over-time ranked game (12 min total game) finish the game with 4 passes and 4 interceptions; with 0 goals, assists, saves, and no disconnects/lag. That's one pass and tackle/deflection every 3 minutes. None of us did anything to provoke this sort of behavior in this person. We were passing the ball to him a lot (unfortunately) because he was open. The game could use more stats showing up on the scoreboard such as turnovers given and shots/shots on goal. Game desperately needs a community over-watch case and report system so the community can police itself and temp ban ball-hogs from "playing" the game. If you want to play this game and have nothing but fun consider what game mode you are going to play and make sure you have enough friends for a full party. As it stands, playing with randoms in this game is an absolutely miserable experience the overwhelming majority of the time.

-/+ Controls I definitely recommend on the pad, to control on the keyboard I totally did not convince myself and had problems with it
+ Potential partnerships with footballers like Ronaldinho, for example, can also make the game more inviting
+ 3v3, 4v4, 5v5 so you can always play in smaller numbers too
- a lot of synchronization errors and stability on servers, which is probably the biggest problem in the game at the moment
+ interesting battlepass system, in which we can choose the path of unlocked rewards

If you are looking for a game to play with friends I highly recommend it. I look forward to improvements and further development by the developers

Me encanta, incomparable a cierta franquicia que también trata de fútbol y recicla su juego anualmente de la cual se excusan por mantener las plantillas actualizadas (ejem).

Las visuales son muy bonitas, incluyendo las animaciones de los chutes y demás, me parecen muy "smooth". Tiene un gameplay difícil de "masterizar", pero que da gusto ir perfeccionando robos de balón, chutes, paradas, pases y regates. A diferencia de anteriores juegos de fútbol, se agradece el hecho de que esté ideado para tomar el control de un único personaje en la formación del equipo, ya que le da un toque de inmersión bastante bueno. Los diferentes estadios también me parecen bastante bonitos, teniendo cada uno su canción de ambiente. 

Respecto a los servidores, noto una mejoría muy alta comparado a los que habian en la beta, ya que la estabilidad de la partida no se pierde en ningún momento (como pasaba en la beta que algunas veces el balón se teletransportaba). Le tengo muchas ganas a subir rango en el competitivo cuando lo desbloquee.

Por el momento las únicas pegas que le puedo poner es que me ha crasheado una vez, cada vez que entro al juego el lenguaje del doblaje se pone de forma automática al inglés, y el hecho de que únicamente puedas desactivar el micrófono en las repeticiones de los goles se me hace algo molesto, ya que podrías haberlo activado sin querer. 

Por último, el hecho de que estoy leyendo que no hay cross-play, cosa que me parece bastante mal ya que todo juego que sale por estas épocas y trata de ser un juego en línea, permite jugar desde diferentes plataformas. Supongo que lo cambiarán tarde o temprano. 

Quitando el tema del cross-play, lo recomiendo totalmente, es muy divertido.
Очень хорошая игра как по мне, динамичная, если проектом занимается фиксить баги то вообще будет прекрасно, надеюсь разработчики не забьют болт

Don't get deterred by the arcadey lights, sounds, stylised graphics, and the fact it's 5vs5. This is as close as it gets to feeling like playing in a football match.

For me, this does what FIFA and PES [b] couldn't [/b] and [b] can't [/b] do and immerse you in a game of football. You're not Agent Smith from the matrix anymore; darting about from one homogeneous puddle-drinking muppet to another, ping pong passing and patronisingly letting you RNG your way through a one on one. They're nothing but TV watching simulators with their dog feces lick of paint of Sky Sports dross. No more nebulous, watered down, utterly limp-wristed and childish amount of statistical nonsense.

You want to play a really solid interpretation of football? [b] Fundamentally, [/b] that is. Play this. Rematch will test what you think you know about playing football as you can't RNG the dreadful away anymore. It's the best interpretation we've got by far and it's probably more than we deserve quite frankly after huffing away at EA's and Konami's arse cheeks for three decades for nothing more than stagnant swamp ass juice.

First off, this game is super fun and I can't wait for them to get it up to a highly polished level. Here are the current issues.

I've got 16 hours in game, I've played in matches for 8 hours. You will spend half of your time waiting for a match currently.
It keeps taking me off of the match stat screen when I want to see how we did. 
You can't instantly restart trainings.
Game crashes. XXXXXXXX
Matchmaking takes a very long time. US-East seems to work ok, but I bet I'm getting more lag issues because of matching with a server further away.
Server lag is a big issue. Two people will go for the ball, it will look like you win the ball so you start running in the direction of the other goal, but the game will lag and suddenly the ball is at the feet of the other player and they're already halfway toward your net.

If they fix these issues, it'll be a great game.

Здесь огромная проблема с неткодом, механиками, багами и огромным количеством кричащих европейцев (если купили - замьюте звук микрофона. Целее будите)
Игра была расфоршена в Запрещенограмме и Ютубе.
Игра была в бете. В нее много кто поиграл. Был плейтест. В него много тоже кто играл. Но это ничего не исправило и в релиз пришла сырая игра. Во время игры было много вопросов, например, как вратарь притянулся к мячу, хотя мяч летит быстрее, чем может развить скорость вратарь даже прыжком. 
Игра НЕ ДЛЯ КЛАВИАТУРЫ И МЫШИ! Вы также как и я будете злиться на кривые пасы, ибо пас зависит от того, куда двигаешься, а не от направления взгляда (Зачем разделили удар и пас по векторам, ААА???)
Это ДВОРОВЫЙ ФУТБОЛ! Здесь нет аута. Почти все играют от стенок. Почувствовать себя футболистом вы здесь не сможете.
Здесь нет разделения на атакующего, защитника и полузащитника. Вратарь - это, по мнению игры, "Свободный защитник". Он может бегать не тратя выносливость до половины поля (Чувствуете нотку бреда?). Если вы сможете договориться с командой насчет ролей - будет прекрасно. Рейтинг здесь 5 на 5, поэтому привыкайте к 10 игрокам на поле, а не 3 на 3.

Да, это почти единственная игра такого типа, но лучше подождите годик, перед тем, как вступать на путь пинателя мячика.

Sloclap’i çoğu kişinin aksine yalnızca Sifu ile değil, Absolver döneminden beri takip ediyorum. Bu oyunun da onların imzasını taşıdığını öğrendiğim andan itibaren Rematch radarımdaydı. Betada yaklaşık 6 saat geçirdim ve yalnızca oradaki keyifli deneyimim bile oyunu ön sipariş etmeme yetti.

Söylenenlerin aksine, hiçbir çökme problemi yaşamadım. Küçük buglar oldu ama hiçbirisi oyunu baltalayacak düzeyde değildi. 

Bazı oyuncular, tek başına oynamanın keyifsiz olduğunu söylüyordu. Elbette arkadaşlarla birlikte oynamak, yapılan hatalara birlikte gülmek çok daha eğlenceli. Ancak rastgele eşleştiğim oyuncularla da gayet keyifli maçlar yaptım; konuşmadan anlaşabildiğimiz, taktik geliştirdiğimiz birçok güzel an yaşadım. Bencil oyuncularla karşılaşmak nadir de olsa mümkün ama genel deneyimi gölgeleyecek boyutta değildi. 

Geliştirici ekibe, lokalizasyon ve açık betalar için teşekkür ediyorum. Önümüzdeki süreçte sadece kozmetik unsurlara odaklanmalarını değil, topluluktan gelen geri bildirimleri de dikkate alarak oyunun tekrar oynanabilirliğini ve verimliliğini korumalarını rica ediyor ve Rematch'in fiyatının hakkını kesinlikle verdiğini düşünüyorum.

Güzel bir iş, tebrikler!

Edit Eylül 2025

Geliştirici ekip süreci kesinlikle yönetemiyor. Biz fix ya da içerik bekledikçe kozmetiğe yüklendiler. Gerçekten hayal kırıklığı oldu. Muazzam bir potansiyeli yok ettiklerini düşünüyorum. O yüzden olumsuz olarak değiştiriyorum.

Me duele dejar una reseña negativa, porque sinceramente el juego me gusta y lo disfruto mucho con mis amigos. La jugabilidad es rápida, adictiva y exige habilidad real; cuando todo funciona bien, la experiencia es excelente. Se nota que hay algo especial detrás: la propuesta mecánica tiene muchísimo potencial y hasta la banda sonora está a otro nivel.

Pero, por más que valore todo eso, no puedo ignorar el modelo de negocio, que me parece pésimamente planteado. Me siento en parte estafado.

Estamos hablando de un juego que cuesta casi 30 €, y aun así incluye micropagos, moneda premium, pase de batalla y una tienda con cosméticos bloqueados. Es absurdo tener que pagar por entrar y luego seguir pagando si quieres acceder a aspectos estéticos que, en la beta abierta, estaban disponibles gratuitamente.

Y ese es precisamente el mayor problema: el personaje que me hice en la beta desapareció (entendible, mi reseña no va por ahí), pero lo que molesta es que elementos cosméticos que antes estaban accesibles ahora están tras un muro de pago. No es una cuestión de progreso perdido como creen algunos, sino de una mala decisión monetaria que convierte contenido gratuito en contenido premium sin justificación real.

Se nota demasiado el enfoque “live service” como excusa para justificar los micropagos. Pero no todos los juegos necesitan una tienda o una divisa premium para sostenerse. Títulos como Elden Ring: Nightreign demuestran que se puede ofrecer contenido de calidad sin recurrir a este tipo de prácticas. Y aunque el pase de batalla “se pague solo”, no compensa el hecho de que uno ya ha pagado el juego y aun así se le pide más.

No pretendo tener la verdad absoluta, pero así es como me siento: aunque me divierta, no puedo recomendar el juego con buena conciencia. Ojalá lo arreglen, porque la base es sólida, pero el enfoque comercial y el estado actual del juego (con crasheos y laggeos incluidos) empañan por completo lo que podría ser una buena experiencia.

Keep releasing your premium slop skins, guys.
We're definitely buying them.
(We're not, and at this point, most of us aren't even playing the damn game either.)

Played a few matches. Laughed. Cried. Yelled at teammates. Classic football.

This game is like Rocket League but with legs — fast-paced, chaotic, and full of moments where you scream:
“WHY ARE YOU CHASING THE BALL, JASON?! STAY IN POSITION!”

Controls feel great when they work… until the ball clips through your leg and warps into the goal.
Sometimes your pass goes to Mars.
Sometimes your goalkeeper is on vacation.

Still — when it clicks, it's super fun. Especially with friends.
Just don’t expect the randoms to know what “defend” means.

Pros: fun, sweaty, unique.
Cons: teleporting balls, solo queue therapy sessions.

Final verdict:
Broken in places. Beautiful in chaos.
Still recommended (with headphones and patience).

EDIT:
After a spotty launch, the game has slowly picked up the pace. Patching out bugs, issues and general crash-fixes. 
Rematch at it's core is a game that intends to put you in the centre of dynamic fast paced football, challenging the choices and actions you make on the fly. I feel like this has been done pretty well.

The issues in Rematch at the moment are definitely netcode and certain overuse of spam actions. Sometimes you'll see the ball hit the back of the net and teleport into the hands of an ill-prepared keeper, you'll retrieve the ball to be met with an armada of torpedo missles spamming the slide tackle button. Both can be pretty frustrating depending on the situation.
However, when the game works as intended it does so very well. The linkup passing playstyle has been the most satisfying I've ever experienced in any football game to date. When you and your team manage to come together to dominate the midfield allowing you to produce a potent attacking opportunity it feels fluid and most of all, rewarding.

Rematch is now sitting at a point of opportunity and how they adapt the game will directly change how the playerbase responds. The community is very vocal about what they want so I imagine the team knows exactly how they should adapt certain elements to respond. My main point would definitely be a competitive 3v3 mode, something that gives you a rapid game type that allows you to still play in that ranked format without having so many uncontrollable variables, basically a perfect blend of casual/competitive with the opportunity to spiral an entirely new branch of competitiveness in this limited format where every action influences the outcome of your game. 

Overall, they've changed my review to a positive one, and that alone shows they're going in the right direction. Rematch has a lot of potential and now is the time to capture it.

In its current state its rough around the edges but incredibly fun and still a solid 8/10.

Pro:
Feels a lot like fifa street from back in the day. Emphasis on quick decisive plays, flashy moves, and overall fun atmosphere. Very easy to pick up and play right out the gate, no need to dump 2000 hours into it like rocket league to learn all the nooks and crannies (as long as you play the tutorial and actually learn). Ball control is fluid and feels responsive, shooting is fun with variations in curve and height. Customized characters add a touch of personality to each player. Very fun with friends but even playing solo can be fun as well, roughly every 1/6 matches you will meet toxic teammates though so just let them yap. 

Con:
The servers are rather touchy in the games current state, random disconnects here and there, crashes mid-match after scoring a goal (very rare) and the intermittent desync between players that causes some very odd/frustrating "MF HOWWWW" moments. Currently not a lot of players seem to understand the mechanics/ how to pass the ball (at all) and try to out-dribble a whole team at once which can be frustrating at times, however the game is new so we're all learning anyways. 

Overall:
Solid game to pickup and enjoy with friends or solo for a good while, quick matches that last roughly 7 minutes on average so pick-up play is available. Within a few updates I assume things will smooth out in terms of connectivity and desync. Fun and flashy football game that reminds me of back when sports games were fun.

I am Prof. Dr. Joao Vitor, a medical researcher and scientist. As someone who owns and has appreciated all previous games developed by Sloclap and previous abandoned too, I must express my deep disappointment with Rematch and the Sloclap Company.

The game suffers from numerous issues:

A. Frequent crashes, making it nearly unplayable at times, forcing computer to restart playing risking suing process.
B. Oversimplified mechanics, likely to appeal to casual users, but it severely undermines the skill-based nature expected in a competitive soccer game.
C. A lack of quality customization options there are very few appealing skins or items.
D. An ineffective matchmaking system with no proper skill-based balancing.
E. The price is unreasonably high. For what’s offered, the game should be, at most, R$30. As an indie studio, releasing a title at such a high price without meeting fundamental standards is not only unwise but ethically questionable.

In conclusion, Rematch does not live up to the expectations set by Sloclap’s previous titles. It currently lacks the quality, stability, and value to justify a purchase. Based on my experience, I advise others not to waste their time or money on this release.

Regards,
Prof. Dr. Joao Vitor

Remath é uma grata surpresa no cenário dos jogos de futebol.
A jogabilidade é única, fluida e viciante — depois que você pega o ritmo, as horas passam sem perceber. É divertido de verdade, e isso mostra que um bom jogo não depende de gráficos ultra realistas, mas sim de mecânicas bem pensadas e diversão de verdade.

A customização dos acessórios e cores também é um toque muito legal, dá personalidade aos personagens e deixa a experiência mais envolvente.

O único ponto negativo no momento é a demora para encontrar partidas. Isso pode atrapalhar quem está chegando agora, e talvez um preço mais acessível ajudasse a atrair mais jogadores e manter a comunidade ativa.

No geral, vale a pena experimentar, principalmente se você curte jogos competitivos com uma pegada diferente.

I want to like this game, but my unbiased opinion is it needs work. If you're going to full release the game with this many bugs for the price tag it has, it makes me want to give a negative review. DO NOT GET ME WRONG, THIS GAME IS FUN WHEN IT WORKS. Many have probably mentioned already that the desync is a major problem. Even when setting your preferred server to your time zone / region, you're sitting at around 100ms some games, and it's a vibe killer when people are just lagging through you on defense, you can't get a touch off sometimes on offense, the shot goes right through your hands in goal. I have a strong sense that this game will die in a few months like Battlebit Remastered did. There's not much more you can add to a sports game besides cosmetics and new arenas. I look forward to where the devs take this, and I don't regret purchasing, but this game needs some serious work done. Releasing in Early Access for a lesser price would've been a better move in my opinion.

Receta pastel balón de fútbol, para amantes del esférico y el chocolate:

INGREDIENTES:
Para 10 personas
Chocolate negro (para la mousse de chocolate) 350 g
Huevo (para la mousse de chocolate) 6
Mantequilla sin sal (para la mousse de chocolate) 75 g
Azúcar 3 cucharadas (para la mousse de chocolate)
Huevo (para los bizcochos) 8
Azúcar (para los bizcochos) 200 g
Harina de trigo (para los bizcochos) 250 g
Levadura química 2 cucharaditas (para los bizcochos)
Cacao en polvo (para los bizcochos)
Agua (para el almíbar) 150 ml
Azúcar (para el almíbar) 150 ml
Ron (licor) un chorrito (para el almíbar)

Cómo hacer pastel balón de fútbol:
Tiempo total ->2 h 30 m
Elaboración -> 1 h 45 m
Cocción -> 45 m
Reposo -> 1 h

Empezamos preparando la mousse de chocolate, para que tenga tiempo de endurecerse mientras hacemos el bizcocho y el almíbar. Para ello, fundimos el chocolate y la mantequilla al baño María. Retiramos el cazo del fuego y colocamos la mezcla en un bol, añadiendo los huevos uno a uno y batiendo bien después de agregar cada uno.

Aparte, montamos las claras a punto de nieve. Cuando estén fuertes, añadimos el azúcar y batimos un poco más. Por último, mezclamos las claras con la mezcla de chocolate realizando movimientos envolventes, para que no se baje. Guardamos la mousse en la nevera mientras continuamos con el pastel.

Para hacer los bizcochos, montamos las claras a punto de nieve firme y las reservamos. En otro bol, batimos las yemas con el azúcar hasta que quede una mezcla lisa. Añadimos la harina y la levadura tamizadas, mezclando bien. Por último agregamos las claras a punto de nieve, integrando todo mediante movimientos envolventes.

Separamos la masa en dos boles, la misma cantidad aproximadamente en cada uno. En una de las mitades añadimos el cacao, nos quedará una masa blanca y otra marrón. Colocamos cada mitad en una bandeja distinta de horno, untada con mantequilla y forrada con papel de cocina. Por último, horneamos cada bandeja, por separado, unos 12 minutos a 180 ºC.

Una vez horneados los dos bizcochos, los dejamos enfriar. A continuación, del bizcocho blanco recortamos diez hexágonos. Del bizcocho chocolateado recortamos cinco pentágonos y un círculo tan grande como el molde que vayamos a usar, que nos servirá de base para el pastel. Las figuras que usé tenían cinco centímetros de lado.

Para hacer el almíbar, ponemos el azúcar, el agua y el ron en un cazo y los dejamos hervir unos ocho o diez minutos. Una vez listo el almíbar lo dejamos enfriar, mientras empezamos a montar el pastel.

Forramos una ensaladera con papel film y vamos disponiendo en ella los hexágonos y pentágonos de bizcocho, intercalando para ir haciendo el dibujo característico de un balón de fútbol, como puede verse en las fotografías del paso a paso. Con un pincel de cocina, embadurnamos bien el bizcocho con el almíbar, reservando un poco para humedecer la base.

Sacamos la mousse de chocolate que teníamos reservada y rellenamos el bizcocho. Por último, disponemos encima la base de bizcocho, que previamente habremos embebido con el resto de almíbar. Tapamos con papel film y reservamos en la nevera hasta el momento de servir.

Para finalizar, a disfrutar :)

По началу игра будет казаться классной, но чем дольше в нее играешь тем больше проблем замечаешь.
1. Кривой неткод, дисинк тут обыденность
2. Сервера в Европе не разделены на Запад и Восток
3. Приоритет действий у того у кого пинг ниже, что сильно влияет на баланс игры на высоком уровне
4. Матчмейкинг самый убогий что можно встретить. Ранг Мастер или Элита должны играть с сильвер тимейтами которые ничего не могут против фул стаков. И не важно соло ты ищешь или дуо игра просто не делит людей никак просто общий котел страдай как хочешь.
5. Тимейты зачастую либо просто тупые либо наглухо отбитые которые пересмотрели Блю Лок, поэтому если у вас нет хотя бы 4 своих человек вам обеспечен незабываемый опыт людской тупости
У игры большой потенциал но боюсь даже за год они не смогут решить свои проблемы. Со стороны разрабов можно понять почему игра вышла в таком виде так как после беты пока на хайпе и удачный промежуток времени для выпуска, так как скоро новая фифа для лудаманов . А игроки получили новый экспиренс на недельку, а дальше они просто осознали на сколько же игра сырая.
Crossplay yok (Gamepass ile bile), sürekli oyundan düşüyorsunuz maçta, ses sıkıntıları çok bol. Düşünce güzel ama teknik olarak daha yeterli seviyenin çok uzağında biraz bekleyin derim

I've been playing the game since the beta. The beta itself proved that the game had great potential. However, when it came out, cross-play wasn't the first thing that should have been added to a game like this. That's gone now, but the problems continue. Yes, I understand that the developers have no experience with online play, but I can't stand that a game that could have been this perfect is this bad. I can't stand stupidity. I apologize for my harsh words, but the season rewards are really bad. Getting past that, the game starts to get repetitive after a while. One of the first two things that should have been added after crossplay is team building, and the other is that instead of offering clothing variety to the paying audience, they impose this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ captain pass on us. I could pay, but that's not the point. In a game that I've already paid for, and where there's little variety, things to do, and objectives, wouldn't it be nice to at least make the clothes available for purchase? Going crazy just by playing matches for 5-6 minutes and dealing with some idiots is already achievable.



I just get relegated and promoted, and that doesn't really bring anything in. So, shouldn't I be given something to aim for in a game where I can't win anything but constantly pour in money? Yes, we use games to have fun and escape the stress of daily life, but rematch occasionally fail in this regard with some players. That's not my complaint, of course, but wouldn't it be nice if you at least made content unlockable that would give the game a feeling of "accomplishment"? In fact, there were people who didn't play until crossplay came along for that very reason.

Don't get me wrong, I'm someone who patiently plays and exhausts every opportunity, giving every game its all. But this is the first time I've been so questioning. I've never seen a roadmap that so completely erodes its potential.

Bu oyun kesinlikle harika!

Grafikler 9/10. Her detay çok özenle işlenmiş, görsel anlamda etkileyici bir atmosfer sunuyor. Özellikle ışıklandırma ve çevre tasarımları çok başarılı. Tekrar söylemek gerekirse, biraz daha fazla detay olsa 10/10 olurdu ama yine de göz alıcı bir grafik deneyimi sunuyor.

Oynanış 10/10. Mekanikler gerçekten kusursuz. Kontroller çok akıcı ve her hareket çok doğru bir şekilde tepki veriyor. Özellikle çevresel etkileşimler ve karakterlerin farklı animasyonları çok tatmin edici. Her şey tam yerli yerinde.

Ses tasarımı de oldukça başarılı. Müzikler ve efektler atmosferi güçlendiriyor, sesler oldukça doğal ve gerçekçi. Hatta zaman zaman oyun içinde kaybolup gitmek istiyorsunuz.

Teknik açıdan da müthiş bir deneyim. FPS stabil, yükleme süreleri çok kısa ve oyun neredeyse hiç takılmıyor. Online oyunlarda da kararlılık gerçekten harika.

Sonuç olarak, bu oyun her yönüyle keyif verici bir deneyim sunuyor ve çok uzun saatler boyunca eğlenebileceğiniz bir oyun. Eğer bir rematch şansı bulursam, mutlaka tekrar oynarım. Kesinlikle tavsiye ediyorum!"

Игра отличная, задумка крутая, исполнено супер, за исключением технических проблем. Играется, особенно в пати, очень круто. 

Но, к сожалению, минусы в игре при этом таких огромных размеров, что отбивается любое желание играть. Хрен с ним с кривым неткодом, который, к слову, после патча с "улучшением" как-будто только хуже стал. Хрен с ним что в игре нет какой-либо весомой прогресии и мотивации играть. 

Самая большая проблема этой игры - ранкед. Его система просто не работает в реалиях этой игры. Игра УЛЬТРА - командозависимая, и поэтому играя соло про ранкед можно вообще забить. Я катал в кучу соревновательных игр, 99% времени соло, и прктически во всех брал высокие ранги, даймонд, мастер и выше, входил в 1-3% лучших игроков. Но ранкед тут - это худший ранкед их всех что я видел. За 40 часов в игре я даже с бронзы вылезти не могу, будучи каждый матч или мвп или как минимум вторым игроком по счёту в команде. Вам будет балансить таких мутантов, которые даже с базовыми кнопками и механиками не разобрались, но зато уже разобрались как зайти в ранговые игры.

И при этом игра абсолютно никак не ценит ваш импакт, выиграл +20, проиграл -16. То есть весь ранкед в игре строится на том "Хоть бы мне повезло сейчас с тиммейтами", ваш личный вклад ни на что не влияет и никак не ценится игрой.

Критикуешь - предлагай. Предлагаю, в ранговых матчах игра должна в большей степени учитывать то, как играет конкретный игрок, а не то, выиграл он или нет. Не сильно шарю в футболе, но насколько я знаю рекрутеры там смотрят на импакт и уровень конкретного игрока, а не на то сосет его команда или нет. Так и тут должно быть. Игра должна давать больше поинтов, если вы играете выше среднего либо набрали хороший счет. И точно так же снимать меньше, если вы отыграли отлично. В настоящий момент тут нет ничего, весь ранкед в соло строится на везении. В идеале, поинты вообще почти полностью должны определяться по уровню и результативности игрока. При этом соло и групповой подборы матчей даже не разделены. 

По итогу мы имеем отличную игру, в которой соло делать тупо нечего. Квики катать соло неинтересно, потому что там совсем новички играют и хорошие матчи случаются крайне редко. Ранкеды - тупо бессмысленны, и работают чисто на удаче, никакой прогрессии или влияния личного импакта. Играбельно исключительно если вы в пати. И вот остается таким образом не классный соревновательный футбольный сим, а чисто развлекуха на вечер в одного или с друзьями. 

А судя по тому как долго чешутся разработчики, исправлений или хоть каких-то улучшений этих систем ждать еще очень долго. Но в этом, конечно, нет их вины, студия просто маленькая.
1000 crashes every day

Let me begin by saying that this game itself is great, in terms of gameplay at least.

The reason why I can't recommend the game is because of technical issues currently present. I am sure this will be fixed at some point hopefully but as of now the game usually crashes about once every 3 matches if not more often.

Not to mention the fact that very often when you crash, in order to play, you need to login to their servers again. This does not work very well, as you'll need to try about 30 times, each one taking anywhere from 10 seconds to a few minutes.

Once they fix these issues I'm sure it will be a great game. It is a great shame, I was really looking forward to this game but I am stuck on being disconnected.

TLDR: Game is good, wait until they fix technical issues.
MESSI MESSI MESSI

I will keep this short and sweet. This is the best sports game that I have played in probably 10 years. It scratches the competitive itch while also reminding me of the old FIFA/NBA/NFL STREET games from the early 2000s. It’s fair, with very few gimmicks or power ups, and getting things right feels just as satisfying as playing soccer in real life. Everyone’s character is on an even playing field, with no “meta builds”, overleveling, or p2w abilities. So if you do something cool, you and everyone else in the lobby will know how cool it was and that it was all you. But it’s also super fun, with simple, yet refined controls and a very high skill ceiling. Coordinating with others is a blast(when they also decide to play team ball lol) and it looks beautiful. I played all 3 betas and purchased the early access bundle from them. So safe to say I have a lot of faith in this game and maybe even a hint of bias. It’s not without its flaws, notably the servers haven’t been great since official launch, and there are some crashing issues on certain PCs. The training modes are there, but need some tuning (restart button PLEASE). The issues I find with the game are all system related—the core gameplay, in my opinion, is a masterpiece and why I can still recommend this game on launch. Even through the crashing and desyncs I had the most fun Ive had in a game in a very long time. As this game receives some more polish on the backend It’ll only get better. Definitely pick it up asap. 

PS. Please Do not add playstyles/skill trees etc. but maybe make headers a bit more intentional/intuitive?
